name: markdown-pdf-plus
description: Export Markdown as PDF or HTML. You can include stylesheets and images, both local and remote.

markdown-pdf-plus.marginTop.markdownDescription:
The width of the top margin of pages for exported PDFs. 

 Available units include `px`, `cm` and `in`. See Mozilla's documentation on [length units](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/length#absolute_length_units) for more options.

markdown-pdf-plus.marginBottom.markdownDescription:
The width of the bottom margin of pages for exported PDFs. 

 Available units include `px`, `cm` and `in`. See Mozilla's documentation on [length units](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/length#absolute_length_units) for more options.

markdown-pdf-plus.marginLeft.markdownDescription:
The width of the left margin of pages for exported PDFs. 

 Available units include `px`, `cm` and `in`. See Mozilla's documentation on [length units](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/length#absolute_length_units) for more options.

markdown-pdf-plus.marginRight.markdownDescription:
The width of the right margin of pages for exported PDFs. 

 Available units include `px`, `cm` and `in`. See Mozilla's documentation on [length units](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/length#absolute_length_units) for more options.

markdown-pdf-plus.pageSize.markdownDescription: The size of pages for exported PDFs. See the [documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@page/size) for the size CSS at-rule descriptor, used with the `@page` at-rule, for more information.

markdown-pdf-plus.CSSPath.markdownDescription: The path of the CSS file you wish to use to style exported documents.

markdown-pdf-plus.CSSRaw.markdownDescription:
The raw CSS you wish to use to style exported documents. 

 E.g., `body { color: red; }`.

markdown-pdf-plus.outputHome.markdownDescription:
The path of the directory where you wish your exported file to be created (without trailing slash). 

 If empty, defaults to the directory of the currently-open file when a relevant command is called.

markdown-pdf-plus.outputFilename.markdownDescription:
The filename under which you wish your exported file to be created (without any given extension). 

 If empty, defaults to the name of your input file.

markdown-pdf-plus.usePageStyleFromCSS.markdownDescription: Give any [@page](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/@page) at-rule declared in CSS priority over the extension's default page settings.

markdown-pdf-plus.chromiumPath.markdownDescription: Path to a Chromium or Chrome executable for PDF rendering. Used instead of the auto-detected bundled Chromium. Only configurable in user/machine settings (not workspace).

markdown-pdf-plus.puppeteerSandbox.markdownDescription:
Chromium sandbox mode for PDF rendering.

- `auto` (default): Tries sandboxed first, falls back to unsandboxed.
- `on`: Always sandboxed (may fail on some Linux systems).
- `off`: Always runs without sandbox (`--no-sandbox`).

markdown-pdf-plus.allowExternalResources.markdownDescription: Allow embedding images and stylesheets from outside the workspace folder during export. Disable when working with untrusted content to prevent local file reads.

markdown-pdf-plus.tableStyle.markdownDescription: Table styling preset for PDF output. **none**: page-break handling only, **minimal**: light bottom borders, **bordered**: full grid borders, **striped**: bordered with alternating row colors.

markdown-pdf-plus.pdfTimeout.markdownDescription: Maximum time in seconds to wait for PDF generation to complete. Increase for complex documents with many images or Mermaid diagrams.

scripts.pretest: yarn run compile && yarn run lint
